summary: Full end-to-end testing of AYANA-BOT (React + FastAPI + MongoDB). Backend: 24/24 pytest tests pass covering health, auth (register/login/admin/duplicate/invalid-phone), full onboarding chain (child->parent->schedule->checkout->activate), schedule mode limits (normal caps at 5, care_plus allows 10, empty rejected), parents CRUD, schedules toggle/delete, messages preview/logs, admin (stats/users/messages/emergencies + role gating), account delete. Frontend: Landing (hero+CTA+FAQ accordion), signup->onboarding 5-step wizard, dashboard tabs (Parents add/edit/delete via dialog, Schedules toggle/edit, Activity, Account delete), admin dashboard (stats + Users/Deliveries/Emergencies tabs), legal pages (/privacy, /terms, /disclaimer) all render correctly. WhatsApp send is simulated (WHATSAPP_ENABLED=false) and payments skipped (PAYMENTS_ENABLED=false) as intended.

issues: Empty state shows correctly but has no data-testid on the empty container; only data-testid='activity-list' appears once logs exist. Test scripts that wait on activity-list will time out for new users. Consider adding data-testid='activity-empty' or wrapping the tab content in a stable testid.

action_items: (Optional/minor) Add a stable data-testid on the Dashboard Activity tab container that is always rendered (currently activity-list only appears when logs > 0).
critical_code_review_comments: backend/models.py: `ScheduleInput.limit_messages` field_validator is a no-op (returns v as-is); the actual mode-based limit is enforced only inside the route. Consider using a model_validator to keep validation with the schema.
backend/server.py: `whatsapp/webhook` uses `parent['user_id']` (already a string) but `parent_id` stored as ObjectId in `parent_replies` is fine; no bug found.

backend/auth.py: `seed_admin` correctly resets admin password if ADMIN_PASSWORD changed. bcrypt used with $2b$ hashes. JWT-only auth (no httpOnly cookie set on login). Per spec this is a JWT-in-localStorage design (documented), so acceptable; the /logout endpoint deletes a cookie that is never set — dead code but harmless.

backend/server.py: /api/parents/{parent_id} PUT does not require deleted_at is null filter — a previously-deleted parent could be resurrected via PUT. Low risk; consider adding {'deleted_at': None} to the find_one.

CORS: `allow_credentials=True` with `allow_origins=['*']` (from CORS_ORIGINS='*') is a browser-invalid combination; browsers will reject credentialed requests. Since the frontend uses Bearer tokens not cookies, it does not break the app, but this is a config smell — set explicit origins in production.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@ayana.care / AyanaAdmin@2026 (works). Test users created via /api/auth/register with @example.com (@*.test blocked by email-validator).
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_-prefixed users/parents/schedules were created in mongodb during testing. Cleanup not performed but data is prefixed for easy identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All V1 flows verified end-to-end against the preview URL. Backend pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_ayana_api.py (24 tests, LoadScopeScheduling, 2 workers). Emails must use non-reserved TLDs (e.g. @example.com) because pydantic EmailStr rejects @.test / @.local. WhatsApp status='simulated' in activation results is expected (WHATSAPP_ENABLED=false).
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found. Initial test failures were due to my own use of @ayana.test emails which pydantic EmailStr rejects; fixed by switching to @example.com.
